title: Run containers as non-root + least privilege
description: Containers run under a non-root user; no privileged mode; drop unnecessary Linux capabilities; set read-only FS where feasible.
evidence_expected: Dockerfile USER; runtime config (compose/k8s); capability drop; securityContext (if k8s); rationale for any exception.
references_frameworks: FW_ISO27001:Technological controls (secure configuration / least privilege); FW_NIS2:Cybersecurity risk-management measures; FW_ASVS:Secure configuration / deployment guidance

title: Use minimal, pinned base images (no 'latest')
description: Base images are minimal (slim/distroless where possible) and pinned by version/digest; 'latest' tag is forbidden.
evidence_expected: Dockerfile FROM pinned; policy doc; CI rule that rejects latest/unpinned; inventory of approved bases.
references_frameworks: FW_NIS2:Supply-chain security; FW_DORA:ICT risk management (supply chain / secure configuration); FW_ISO27001:Supplier relationships / secure configuration

title: Container vulnerability scanning in CI (gate)
description: Every build triggers container image scanning; pipeline fails on High/Critical vulnerabilities unless explicitly risk-accepted.
evidence_expected: CI logs + scan reports (Trivy/Grype/Snyk); policy thresholds; risk-acceptance workflow + expiry.
references_frameworks: FW_DORA:ICT risk management + vulnerability management; FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures; FW_ISO27001:Vulnerability management / technical monitoring

title: SBOM generation + dependency scanning
description: Generate SBOM per build and scan application dependencies (pip/npm/etc.) in addition to image scanning.
evidence_expected: SBOM artifact (CycloneDX/SPDX); dependency scan report; remediation PRs; release evidence.
references_frameworks: FW_NIS2:Supply-chain security; FW_DORA:ICT risk management; FW_ISO27001:Change management / secure development

title: Runtime container security monitoring
description: Detect and alert on suspicious runtime activity (unexpected shell, privilege escalation, anomalous syscalls/network).
evidence_expected: Runtime tool config (Falco/Sysdig/Aqua etc.); alert rules; sample alerts; runbook + response actions.
references_frameworks: FW_DORA:ICT risk management (detect/respond); FW_NIS2:Incident handling + monitoring; FW_GDPR32:Security of processing (integrity/confidentiality)

title: Authentication defined and enforced (OIDC/JWT/mTLS)
description: API authentication mechanism is defined, enforced on all protected endpoints, and documented (tokens, TTL, refresh, signing).
evidence_expected: Architecture doc; gateway/app config; token claims model; sample requests; tests for auth-required endpoints.
references_frameworks: FW_ASVS:V2 Authentication; FW_ISO27001:Access control; FW_GDPR32:Confidentiality

title: Authorization (RBAC/ABAC) + least privilege
description: Authorization model is defined (roles/permissions); access is least-privilege; admin actions are isolated.
evidence_expected: Permissions matrix; enforcement points; tests; admin route protection; periodic access review process.
references_frameworks: FW_ASVS:V4 Access Control; FW_ISO27001:Access control / privilege management; FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures

title: API key lifecycle management
description: API keys are issued per client, stored/handled as secrets, rotated, revoked, and audited; no hardcoding.
evidence_expected: Key issuance/rotation/revocation procedure; secrets manager usage; audit logs of key usage; emergency revoke playbook.
references_frameworks: FW_ISO27001:Cryptographic controls / key management; FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures; FW_ASVS:Credential storage and handling

title: Rate limiting + abuse protection
description: Rate limiting is enforced (per IP, per API key/client, burst + sustained), returning proper 429 and logging abuse events.
evidence_expected: Gateway/WAF config; limits table; tests; dashboards showing throttling; exception handling for trusted internal calls.
references_frameworks: FW_ASVS:V11 Business Logic / V13 API and Web Services; FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures; FW_DORA:Operational resilience

title: Input validation (schema-first)
description: All inputs are validated via explicit schemas; type/length constraints; reject unknown fields; safe parsing for files.
evidence_expected: OpenAPI/JSON Schema/Pydantic models; validation tests; negative test suite; file upload constraints.
references_frameworks: FW_ASVS:V5 Validation, Sanitization and Encoding; FW_ISO27001:Secure development; FW_GDPR32:Integrity

title: Transport security (TLS) + secure headers
description: TLS enforced end-to-end; strong ciphers; HSTS where appropriate; secure headers for web components.
evidence_expected: TLS config; cert rotation process; security headers list; external scan results (optional).
references_frameworks: FW_GDPR32:Confidentiality; FW_ISO27001:Network security / cryptography; FW_ASVS:V9 Communications

title: API security testing (automated + manual)
description: Security tests cover authz bypass, injection, rate limit bypass, and common OWASP API Top 10 patterns.
evidence_expected: Security test plan; automated tests in CI; manual verification checklist; findings + remediation links.
references_frameworks: FW_ASVS:Verification process; FW_DORA:Operational resilience testing; FW_ISO27001:Secure development lifecycle

title: Security event logging enabled (auth, admin, key use, config changes)
description: Log authentication attempts, token/key usage, admin actions, permission changes, and config changes with correlation IDs.
evidence_expected: Log taxonomy; sample log lines; correlation ID propagation; access controls for logs; masking rules.
references_frameworks: FW_GDPR32:Ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality/integrity; FW_NIS2:Incident handling + monitoring; FW_ISO27001:Logging and monitoring

title: Centralized log collection + retention policy
description: Logs are centralized (ELK/Loki/Splunk/etc.), time-synchronized, protected from tampering, and retained per policy.
evidence_expected: Log pipeline diagram; retention settings; access control; backup of logs; time sync configuration (NTP).
references_frameworks: FW_DORA:ICT risk management (monitoring and auditability); FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures; FW_GDPR32:Security of processing

title: Forensic readiness: export logs on request + analysis notes
description: Ability to export logs (filtered by time/tenant/request) and provide relevant analysis notes for forensics.
evidence_expected: Export procedure; access workflow; redaction rules; sample exported bundle; analyst note template.
references_frameworks: FW_DORA:Incident management and reporting; FW_NIS2:Incident reporting and handling; FW_ISO27001:Incident management / evidence handling

title: Sensitive data masking in logs
description: Logs must not store secrets, tokens, passwords, full PAN/PII; use masking/hashing where needed; access is controlled.
evidence_expected: Masking rules; unit tests; example log output; access control list; periodic review.
references_frameworks: FW_GDPR32:Confidentiality; FW_ISO27001:Information classification / access control

title: Security monitoring dashboards + alerting
description: Dashboards and alerts exist for auth failures, spikes, throttling, error rates, runtime alerts, and key security KPIs.
evidence_expected: Grafana/dashboard links; alert rules; on-call routing; runbooks; sample alert history.
references_frameworks: FW_DORA:ICT risk management (detect/respond); FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures; FW_ISO27001:Monitoring activities

title: WAF / gateway protections (as applicable)
description: Gateway/WAF rules applied for common attacks and abuse patterns; protections are tested and tuned.
evidence_expected: WAF config; rule sets; test evidence; exception list; change log.
references_frameworks: FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures; FW_ASVS:V13 API and Web Services

title: Patch management policy + cadence
description: Defined cadence for OS/base image/dependency patching; emergency patch process for critical CVEs.
evidence_expected: Patch policy; schedule; evidence of applied updates; exception approvals with expiry.
references_frameworks: FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures; FW_DORA:ICT risk management; FW_ISO27001:Change management / secure configuration

title: Vulnerability triage + risk acceptance workflow
description: Process to triage findings, assign owners, set SLAs, track remediation, and time-bound risk acceptance with review.
evidence_expected: Triage procedure; SLA matrix; sample tickets; risk acceptance record template.
references_frameworks: FW_ISO27001:Risk treatment; FW_DORA:ICT risk management

title: MFA for admin access and privileged operations
description: MFA required for admin consoles and privileged actions; break-glass procedures defined.
evidence_expected: IdP policy; screenshots/config export; break-glass runbook; audit logs showing enforcement.
references_frameworks: FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures; FW_ISO27001:Access control; FW_ASVS:V2 Authentication

title: Access reviews and least privilege (periodic)
description: Periodic review of roles/permissions; remove stale access; document approvals.
evidence_expected: Review cadence; access review records; offboarding checklist; role catalog.
references_frameworks: FW_ISO27001:Access control / identity lifecycle; FW_DORA:ICT governance

title: Incident response runbooks + severity classification
description: Defined severity levels, runbooks, comms plan, and responsibilities; includes security incidents and outages.
evidence_expected: IR policy; runbooks; comms templates; postmortem template; drill records.
references_frameworks: FW_DORA:ICT incident management and reporting; FW_NIS2:Incident reporting/handling; FW_ISO27001:Incident management

title: Incident reporting readiness (evidence and timelines)
description: Ability to produce incident reports with required details, timelines, impacted services/tenants, and corrective actions.
evidence_expected: Incident report template; evidence checklist; sample completed report; mapping to internal ticket/postmortem.
references_frameworks: FW_DORA:Reporting requirements; FW_NIS2:Reporting requirements

title: Backups + restore tests
description: Backups are automated, encrypted, and restore tests are performed regularly with documented results.
evidence_expected: Backup configuration; schedule; restore test reports; RPO/RTO targets; encrypted storage proof.
references_frameworks: FW_GDPR32:Restore availability and access; FW_DORA:Operational resilience; FW_ISO27001:Backup / continuity

title: Disaster recovery plan + drills (as applicable)
description: DR plan exists with defined RTO/RPO; drills validate procedures and identify gaps.
evidence_expected: DR plan; drill schedule; results; remediation actions; architecture diagram showing failure domains.
references_frameworks: FW_DORA:Resilience testing and continuity; FW_NIS2:Business continuity and crisis management; FW_ISO27001:Continuity planning

title: Secure SDLC policy + code review + CI gates
description: Secure development policy exists; mandatory code review; CI gates for tests, scans, and quality checks.
evidence_expected: SSDLC policy doc; PR review rules; CI pipeline config; sample PR with approvals and passing gates.
references_frameworks: FW_ISO27001:Secure development; FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures; FW_DORA:ICT governance

title: Security testing cadence (SAST/DAST as feasible)
description: Static and/or dynamic testing is performed regularly; findings are tracked to closure.
evidence_expected: Tool outputs; schedules; issue tracker links; remediation evidence.
references_frameworks: FW_ASVS:Verification approach; FW_ISO27001:Testing and evaluation

title: Third-party risk: inventory + contract/security requirements
description: Maintain inventory of third parties (hosting, libraries, providers) and define security expectations and review cadence.
evidence_expected: Vendor inventory; risk ratings; contract clauses list; review records; exception process.
references_frameworks: FW_DORA:ICT third-party risk management; FW_NIS2:Supply-chain security; FW_ISO27001:Supplier relationships

title: Encryption at rest for sensitive data (as applicable)
description: Sensitive data at rest is encrypted (DB disk encryption and/or column-level where needed); keys are managed securely.
evidence_expected: DB/storage encryption settings; key management notes; evidence of key rotation (if applicable).
references_frameworks: FW_GDPR32:Encryption/pseudonymisation (where appropriate); FW_ISO27001:Cryptography

title: Secrets management (no secrets in code/images)
description: Secrets are stored in a secrets manager or secure env/OS store; never committed to repo or baked into images.
evidence_expected: Secrets manager config; secret scanning in CI; rotation procedure; sample evidence of removed secret from history (if occurred).
references_frameworks: FW_ISO27001:Access control / cryptography; FW_NIS2:Risk-management measures
